merger_name: GPS Energy – Ryowa's interest in Gladstone Power Station Joint Venture
merger_description:
GPS Energy Pty Limited (**GPS Energy**), a wholly owned indirect subsidiary of Rio Tinto Aluminium (Holdings) Limited (**RTAHL**), proposes to acquire the 7.125% minority interest held by Ryowa II GPS Pty. Limited (**Ryowa**) in the Gladstone Power Station Joint Venture (**GPS JV**). The GPS JV is an unincorporated joint venture that owns and operates the Gladstone Power Station (**GPS**), a coal-fired power station located in Gladstone, Queensland. The GPS generates electricity that is primarily supplied to Boyne Island Aluminium Smelter (**Smelter**), as well as the National Electricity Market via its market facing participant, CS Energy Limited, a Queensland government owned corporation.

The GPS JV currently has 7 participants:

* GPS Energy (22.125%),
* GPS Power Pty. Limited (20.00%) (also a wholly owned subsidiary of RTAHL),
* Sunshine State Power B.V. (20.00%),
* Sunshine State Power (No. 2) B.V. (17.50%),
* Southern Cross GPS Pty Ltd (8.50%),
* Ryowa (7.125%), and
* YKK GPS (Queensland) Pty Ltd (4.75%).

Following completion of the Acquisition, GPS Energy's interest in the GPS JV will increase from 22.125% to 29.250% (and RTAHL's overall interest will increase from 42.125% to 49.250%), and Ryowa will cease to be a participant in the GPS JV. The remaining participants and their respective interests in the GPS JV will be unchanged.

Rio Tinto Aluminium Limited (**RTAL**) is a wholly owned subsidiary of RTAHL and holds a majority interest in Boyne Smelters Limited (**BSL**), the incorporated joint venture that owns and operates the Smelter. RTAL and RTAHL are subsidiaries of Rio Tinto Limited.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
GPS Energy Pty Limited (GPS Energy), a wholly owned indirect
subsidiary of Rio Tinto Aluminium (Holdings) Limited (RTAHL),
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of the 7.125% minority interest held by Ryowa II GPS Pty.
Limited (Ryowa) in the Gladstone Power Station Joint Venture (GPS
JV), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of
the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The GPS JV is an unincorporated joint venture that owns and
operates the Gladstone Power Station, a coal-fired power station
located in Gladstone, Queensland. The GPS generates electricity that
is primarily supplied to Boyne Island Aluminium Smelter (the
Smelter), as well as the National Electricity Market via its market
facing participant, CS Energy, a Queensland government owned
corporation.
The GPS JV currently has 7 participants:
a. GPS Energy (22.125%),
b. GPS Power Pty. Limited (GPS Power) (20.00%), which is also
a wholly owned subsidiary of RTAHL,
c. Sunshine State Power B.V. (20.00%),
d. Sunshine State Power (No. 2) B.V. (17.50%),
e. Southern Cross GPS Pty Ltd (8.50%),
f. Ryowa (7.125%), and
g. YKK GPS (Queensland) Pty Ltd (4.75%).
Rio Tinto Aluminium Limited (RTAL) is a wholly owned subsidiary of
RTAHL and holds a majority interest in Boyne Smelters Limited, the
incorporated joint venture that owns and operates the Smelter.
RTAHL owns GPS Power, GPS Nominee Pty Limited (which owns
GPS Energy), and RTAL. Both RTAL and RTAHL are subsidiaries of
Rio Tinto Limited.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal consolidation or removal of a
competitor as a result of the Acquisition
b. the Acquisition does not appear to result in any
substantive change in control or day-to-day operation of
the Gladstone Power Station
c. there is low risk of foreclosure or other concerning
vertical effects arising from the Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
